mechanism_narrative: A consistently low unemployment rate signals a robust labor market and strong consumer confidence, which underpins corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The correlation between UNRATE and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is consistently negative or statistically insignificant over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An extremely low federal funds rate indicates an accommodative monetary policy stance, which reduces the cost of capital for businesses and encourages investment, leading to higher equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong growth in retail sales and industrial production signifies broad-based economic expansion and robust demand, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are flat or negative in more than 50% of instances where both RSAFS and INDPRO show strong growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and impending economic contraction, which typically leads to declining corporate profits and negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in periods where UNRATE shows a significant increase during an economic contraction.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in housing starts reflects strong underlying demand and investment in the real estate sector, which has positive ripple effects across the economy and supports equity markets over a short-term horizon.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward returns of the S&P 500 show no statistically significant positive correlation with HOUST growth over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: High and accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real purchasing power and corporate profit margins, leading to expectations of tighter monetary policy and negative sentiment for equity markets over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong employment growth (PAYEMS) coupled with stagnant or declining retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a divergence where labor income is not translating into consumer spending, signaling future economic weakness and negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive or flat when PAYEMS shows growth but RSAFS is stagnant or declining.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery, a strong rebound in industrial production signals increasing capacity utilization and business confidence, which is highly supportive of corporate earnings and equity market performance over a short-term horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) when INDPRO shows strong growth during a recovery period.
